sourceMovie.title: The Croods
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Croods (2013) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Croods clicked for you, Ice Age (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Croods clicked for you, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Croods?
faq.answer: Try Ice Age, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs, The Croods: A New Age—each shares DNA with The Croods while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Croods?
faq.answer: The Croods (2013) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Family, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Croods part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Croods Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
